title: Health Canada educational visit of JTI-Macdonald manufacturing facilities in Montreal, Quebec – November 24, 2022 - Canada.ca
content:
## Health Canada educational visit of JTI-Macdonald manufacturing facilities in Montreal, Quebec – November 24, 2022
Download the alternative format (PDF format, 808 KB, 3 pages)
An educational visit of JTI-Macdonald (hereinafter referred to as "JTI")
  was requested by Health Canada to better understand some technical issues
  raised during the consultation on the proposed Tobacco Products Packaging and Labelling Regulations published in June 2022 in the Canada Gazette , Part I.
- Denis Choinière Director, Tobacco Products Regulatory Office (TPRO),
        Tobacco Control Directorate (TCD), Controlled Substances and Cannabis
        Branch (CSCB)
- Director, Tobacco Products Regulatory Office (TPRO),
        Tobacco Control Directorate (TCD), Controlled Substances and Cannabis
        Branch (CSCB)
- Senior Policy Analyst, Tobacco Labelling, TPRO, TCD, CSCB
- Policy Analyst, Tobacco Labelling, TPRO, TCD, CSCB
- Elaine McKay Head of Corporate Affairs and Communications
- Head of Corporate Affairs and Communications
- Charles Johnston Engineering Manager
An educational site visit of JTI-Macdonald was conducted at the request of
  Health Canada (HC) to better understand some technical issues raised during
  the consultation on the proposed Tobacco Products Packaging and Labelling Regulations published in
  June 2022 in the Canada Gazette , Part I.
HC reminded participants that this meeting is subject to disclosure as per
  HC's Openness and Transparency policies . In the interest of transparency, the Department stated that it would be
  making a record of the meeting publicly available. The handling of information and privacy notice was mentioned and acknowledged.
HC also referred to Article 5.3 of the World Health Organization Framework Convention on Tobacco Control , its international obligation to protect tobacco control policies from the
  vested interests of the tobacco industry. It was acknowledged by JTI
  representatives.
The site visit demonstrated the following:
- Manufacturing and distribution process for regular size and king size
    cigarettes;
- Process to implement changes to tipping paper length for tobacco products
    with a tipping paper of less than 24 mm in length and to display health
    warnings on tipping paper for cigarettes;
- Modifications being considered to cigarette-making machines where the
    tipping paper is less than 24mm in length; and
- Modifications being considered to cigarette packaging machines to
    accommodate slides with an extended upper-slide flap (slide-and-shell
    packs).
The visit was then concluded.
